DISCOVERY 302, 2)-PLAINTIFF DAGO DURAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF'S SECOND MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER OF DEFENDANT AVILLA IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF APPEARS TO HAVE DECIDED TO SKIP OVER OTHER LESS SEVERE MOTIONS SUCH AS MOTIONS FOR ISSUE/EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS, THE MOTION SEEKING THE ULTIMATE, TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS DENIED BECAUSE "SANCTIONS ARE GENERALLY IMPOSED IN AN INCREMENTAL APPROACH, WITH TERMINATING SANCTIONS BEING THE LAST RESORT." DEPT. OF FORESTRY & FIRE PROTECTION V. HOWELL, 18 CAL. APP. 5TH 154, 191 (2017). PLAINTIFF WAITED OVER ONE (1) YEAR AFTER FILING HIS COMPLAINT TO SERVE THE DISCOVERY UNDERLYING THIS MOTION WHICH WAS CALENDARED BY PLAINTIFF TO BE HEARD ON THE VERY LAST DAY DISCOVERY MOTIONS MAY BE HEARD. PLAINTIFF IS NOT PERMITTED TO GAME WITH DEADLINES THAT WERE CREATED BY HIMSELF. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT WILLIAMCLYNN@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM LYNN, CLERK:M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-13-2023 Vacated

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court releases Mandarin Court Interpreter Mimi Lain. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1)-PLAINTIFF DAGO DURAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff's first motion to strike Answer of Defendant Eherenfeldt is denied. Plaintiff appears to have decided to skip over other less severe motions such as motions for issue/evidentiary sanctions, the motion seeking the ultimate, terminating sanctions is denied because "sanctions are generally imposed in an incremental approach, with terminating sanctions being the last resort." Dept. of Forestry & Fire Protection v. Howell, 18 Cal. App. 5th 154, 191 (2017). Plaintiff waited over one (1) year after filing his complaint to serve the discovery underlying this motion which was calendared by Plaintiff to be heard on the very last day discovery motions may be heard. Plaintiff is not permitted to game with deadlines that were created by himself.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk:M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/jpt)

Off Calendar Mar-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DAGO DURAN's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions (Florine Eherenfeldt).

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion is granted. Defendant Eherenfeldt shall respond to Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Requests for Production of Documents and Things, Set One without objection within 15 days.

No sanctions. A request for sanctions must be set forth with specificity in the Motion.

Counsel for plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)
